fields.description:
## Context

Fills the **T2 semantic-resolution tier** deferred out of the code-grounding v1 floor (epic
`8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82`, closed). v1 shipped the deterministic, polyglot, fail-open **T0
(manifest/registry existence) + T1 (ctags/tree-sitter syntactic)** floor plus a **T2
abstain-by-default seam**: `TIER_T2` exists in the closed tier vocabulary but is never emitted,
and member/dotted references abstain inline in `resolve.refute_absence` (tagged "member binding is
T2"). This epic wires a real, self-contained, opt-in T2 backend behind that seam **without changing
the three-valued evidence contract or its consumers**.

The constraints any T2 backend must respect (why T2 was deferred):
- **Not deterministic by nature.** Semantic resolution is a function of machine state (installed
  deps, build state, runtime versions), not just the artifact. Every failure mode
  (not-installed / not-built / wrong-version / hung) is indistinguishable from a genuinely fake
  symbol.
- **Confirm-only verdict asymmetry.** A T2 `present` (in this oracle: `refuted` — an asserted
  absence disproved) is trustworthy; a T2 `absent` is suspect unless the environment is known-good.
  So T2 emits a trustworthy `refuted` and otherwise **abstains** — it never sits on a deterministic
  blocking path.
- **Cannot be borrowed from the ambient environment.** No reliance on an externally-configured MCP
  server (e.g. Serena) or a host toolchain; any backend is self-contained + opt-in.

## Decided v1 design (was a placeholder; now decomposed)

**Selected v1 backend: one-shot compiler/checker-CLI diagnostics** (candidate 2), starting with
`pyright --outputjson` for Python. Rationale over the alternatives: no long-lived server (simplest to
fail-open + batch), no ambient dependency (the checker is an opt-in extra / PATH probe), and it yields
a trustworthy `refuted` when the checker runs cleanly. The **bundled LSP-client library** (candidate
1) and **containerized/hermetic** (candidate 3) tiers are **deferred** — the LSP path multiplies the
per-language server/runtime/config surface, and the containerized path's gating cost is an
egress-locked single-tenant microVM sandbox (arbitrary-code-execution on untrusted client repos). Both
remain future backends behind the same seam this epic establishes.

**Confirm-only mapping (the locked policy):** run the checker over the reference's file; **checker ran
+ file parsed + imports resolved + no diagnostic at the reference → `refuted@T2`** (asserted absence
disproved, trustworthy). **Any diagnostic at the reference, unresolved imports (env-not-built), no
locatable file, unsupported language, missing tool, timeout, or crash → `abstain`** with a closed
structured reason. T2 **never** asserts an absence.

**IaC grounding lane: split out.** The Terraform `validate`/Registry/`plan` tiering is a distinct
domain (not a code-build environment) — it becomes its **own sibling epic**, not a child here.

## Decomposition (3 stacked children, bottom → top)

- **S1 — ADR + IaC split (design/doc):** record the backend selection, the confirm-only mapping, the
  deferral of LSP-lib/containerized, and create the sibling IaC epic.
- **S2 — pluggable T2 resolver seam (default-off):** a `T2Backend` protocol + registry, the
  `grounding.t2_enabled`/`t2_backend` config, `grounding_info` reporting of registered T2 backends,
  and escalation of member/dotted + not-found-symbol references from the T1 lane to the T2 backend
  — default-off so behavior is unchanged and consumers need no edit.
- **S3 — pyright diagnostics backend (Python):** the real backend behind S2's seam, the optional
  `grounding-t2` extra, self-registration into `grounding_info`, docs, and a per-backend fail-open
  matrix.

## Success Criteria

- At least one self-contained, opt-in T2 backend implements the v1 seam without changing the oracle's
  three-valued evidence contract or its consumers.
- T2 emits a trustworthy `refuted` and abstains (`unknown`) otherwise; an absence is only ever implied
  under explicit recorded preconditions, never asserted.
- T2 is never on a deterministic blocking path and is never sourced from the ambient environment.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] S1 lands an ADR selecting the v1 T2 backend (compiler-CLI diagnostics / pyright) with the
      cost/security/determinism tradeoffs recorded, and splits the IaC lane into its own sibling epic.
- [ ] S2 lands the pluggable T2 seam behind the unchanged three-valued contract (outcome + reason +
      provenance tier); default-off; existing consumers require no change (regression-safe).
- [ ] S3 lands a self-contained, opt-in pyright diagnostics backend that emits `refuted@T2` on a clean
      resolve and `abstain` on every flaky/missing/timeout/unsupported/diagnostic case.
- [ ] Confirm-only verdict policy enforced end-to-end: trustworthy `refuted`; no asserted `absent`;
      all failure modes → `abstain` with a closed structured reason.
- [ ] Self-contained + opt-in: no ambient MCP server / host toolchain reliance; gated behind an
      optional extra + off-by-default config; abstains cleanly when prerequisites are absent.
- [ ] Fail-open tested per backend (unsupported lang / no tool / no locatable file / crash / timeout /
      diagnostic-present → abstain).
- [ ] All three children (S1–S3) closed and their changes landed on `main` via the Gerrit stack.
